改完了。兩個都是「Add to Home Screen」會用到的字串:

- `clients/chess-web/public/manifest.webmanifest.tmpl:3` — `short_name` (Android / desktop Chrome 安裝後的 icon label)
- `clients/chess-web/index.html:21` — `apple-mobile-web-app-title` (iOS Safari「加入主畫面」用的標題)

兩處都改為 `David's 象棋`。

幾個提醒:
1. 要重新 build 才會生效 — `make build-web` (server 部署) 或 `make build-web-static WEB_BASE=Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi` (GitHub Pages)。
2. 已經把舊版加到主畫面的裝置不會自動換名 — 需要移除 icon 再重加,或者讓 SW 更新後手動刪除重裝。瀏覽器 cache 的 manifest 也可能要等 SW 換版(build 會 bump `APP_VERSION`,提示 reload toast 後才真正換)。
3. `name`(完整名稱)維持「Chinese Chess 中國象棋」,只有 home-screen 短標題改了 — 如果你也想連安裝對話框 / 分享 metadata 一起改,跟我說一聲我再動。

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/pwa.md`

```text
1	# Rust + WASM as a PWA — chess-web takeaway
2	
3	This is the long-form companion to the brief `CLAUDE.md` gotcha.
4	It captures the architecture decisions, the build flow, the
5	update lifecycle, and every trap we hit while turning the Rust +
6	Leptos + Trunk SPA into an installable, offline-capable PWA on
7	two hosting targets.
8	
9	It is meant to be the document you re-read in a year when you
10	have to debug something here, and as a transferable recipe if
11	you build another Rust/WASM app and want a PWA on top.
12	
13	---
14	
15	## TL;DR
16	
17	- **A PWA is the website + 3 extras**: a manifest, a service
18	  worker, and an icon set. The Rust/WASM bundle stays unchanged.
19	- **The service worker is plain JS** — written by hand, no
20	  Workbox, no npm. Templated and rendered by a Trunk post-build
21	  hook so the precache list is always in sync with the hashed
22	  filenames Trunk just emitted.
23	- **Single template, two deploy targets**. The same hook runs
24	  for `make build-web` (chess-net `--static-dir`, base = `/`) and
25	  `make build-web-static WEB_BASE=Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi` (GitHub
26	  Pages, base = `/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi`). It reads
27	  `TRUNK_PUBLIC_URL` and substitutes `__BASE_PATH__` so the
28	  manifest's `start_url` / `scope` and every precached URL are
29	  correct for that deployment.
30	- **Update UX is "prompt then reload", not auto-reload**. A
31	  fresh build precaches but the old SW keeps controlling the
32	  page; the new one stays in `waiting` until the user clicks the
33	  toast we render. We do not auto-reload because that would
34	  interrupt 對局.
35	- **Online endpoints are bypassed by the SW.** The `/ws*` /
36	  `/lobby` / `/rooms` / `/api/*` routes flow straight to the
37	  network; the existing failure surface is intact.
38	
39	---
40	
41	## File layout
42	
43	```
44	clients/chess-web/
45	  index.html                    # adds <link rel="manifest">,
46	                                # <link rel="apple-touch-icon">,
47	                                # <script defer src="pwa.js">,
48	                                # and Trunk copy-file directives
49	                                # for the public/ assets.
50	  Trunk.toml                    # adds [[hooks]] stage = "post_build"
51	  Cargo.toml                    # adds web-sys features Event,
52	                                # EventTarget, Navigator, CustomEvent
53	  build.rs                      # unchanged — already injects
54	                                # CHESS_WEB_BASE_PATH for routes.rs
55	  scripts/
56	    build-pwa.sh                # Trunk post-build hook
57	    regenerate-placeholder-icons.sh
58	  public/
59	    sw.js.tmpl                  # service worker template
60	    manifest.webmanifest.tmpl   # web app manifest template
61	    pwa.js                      # JS bridge: SW register, install
62	                                # prompt capture, update lifecycle,
63	                                # online detection
64	    icons/
65	      icon.svg                  # source-of-truth design
66	      icon-maskable.svg         # variant cropped for adaptive icons
67	      favicon.svg               # small SVG favicon
68	      icon-192.png              # rasterized placeholder
69	      icon-512.png              # rasterized placeholder
70	      icon-maskable-512.png     # rasterized placeholder
71	      apple-touch-icon-180.png  # iOS home-screen icon
72	  src/
73	    components/pwa.rs           # Leptos UI: PwaState, banner,
74	                                # button, toast, offline indicator
75	    components/mod.rs           # pub mod pwa;
76	    app.rs                      # PwaState::hydrate() + provide_context
77	                                # PwaUpdateToast + OfflineIndicator
78	                                # mounted globally in AppShell
79	    pages/picker.rs             # PwaInstallBanner above the hero
80	    components/sidebar.rs       # PwaInstallButton next to FX toggles
81	  style.css                     # .pwa-banner / .pwa-toast /
82	                                # .pwa-online-dot styles
83	```
84	
85	The PWA glue is intentionally split: anything that needs to fire
86	*before* WASM finishes booting (capturing `beforeinstallprompt`,
87	registering the SW) is in `public/pwa.js`. Everything that needs
88	to render UI is in Rust/Leptos.
89	
90	---
91	
92	## Build flow
93	
94	```
95	trunk build
96	  └─ Rust → app-[hash].js + app-[hash]_bg.wasm
97	  └─ CSS  → style-[hash].css
98	  └─ copy-file (data-trunk rel="copy-file"):
99	       og-image.png
100	       public/sw.js.tmpl       → dist/sw.js.tmpl
101	       public/manifest.webmanifest.tmpl → dist/manifest.webmanifest.tmpl
102	       public/pwa.js           → dist/pwa.js
103	  └─ copy-dir:
104	       public/icons            → dist/icons/
105	
106	post_build hook (scripts/build-pwa.sh):
107	  1. find dist/ for .js/.wasm/.css/.html/.svg/.png/.webmanifest
108	     (skip og-image.png, 404.html, sw.js, *.tmpl)
109	  2. prefix each with TRUNK_PUBLIC_URL → PRECACHE_MANIFEST JSON
110	  3. shasum -a 256 of concatenated file hashes → APP_VERSION
111	  4. awk substitute __APP_VERSION__ / __BASE_PATH__ /
112	     __PRECACHE_MANIFEST__ in templates
113	  5. write dist/sw.js + dist/manifest.webmanifest
114	  6. rm dist/sw.js.tmpl + dist/manifest.webmanifest.tmpl
115	```
116	
117	`TRUNK_PUBLIC_URL` is set by Trunk's `--public-url` flag:
118	
119	| Make target            | `--public-url`               | `BASE_PATH` (in templates) |
120	| ---------------------- | ---------------------------- | -------------------------- |
121	| `make build-web`       | (default `/`)                | `""` (empty)               |
122	| `make build-web-static`| `/$WEB_BASE/`                | `/$WEB_BASE`               |
123	
124	The empty string for the server case is intentional — manifest
125	`start_url: "/"` and `scope: "/"` are written verbatim. For the
126	GitHub Pages case `start_url: "/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/"` and
127	every precache URL becomes `/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/...`.
128	
129	---
130	
131	## Service worker strategy
132	
133	```
134	fetch event:
135	  same origin? └ no  → ignore (let browser fetch)
136	  is /ws, /lobby, /rooms, /api/*? → bypass
137	  navigation? → network-first, fall back to cached index.html
138	  hashed asset (-[8+hex].(js|wasm|css))? → cache-first
139	  other same-origin GET → stale-while-revalidate
140	```
141	
142	**Why network-first for navigations.** SPA route changes (Leptos
143	router) are intercepted at the document level only on the
144	initial load. With network-first we always get a fresh
145	`index.html` when online, and an `index.html`-from-cache when
146	offline → the Leptos router still takes over and renders the
147	right page client-side.
148	
149	**Why cache-first for hashed assets.** Trunk hashes JS/WASM/CSS
150	by content; the filename is its own integrity check. If the
151	filename matches, the bytes are correct, and we can serve from
152	Cache Storage forever. The next build emits a different filename
153	which will miss the cache and be fetched fresh.
154	
155	**Why bypass online endpoints.** chess-net needs to handle
156	disconnection itself; we don't want the SW second-guessing or
157	caching `/lobby` Server-Sent updates.
158	
159	> The PWA install prereq is also what enables LAN multiplayer:
160	> once the service worker is registered, the two phones can
161	> play 1v1 over WebRTC with NO server in the loop. See
162	> [`docs/lan-multiplayer-webrtc.md`](lan-multiplayer-webrtc.md)
163	> for the full pattern.
164	
165	---
166	
167	## Update lifecycle
168	
169	```
170	user has the app open (controlled by SW v1)
171	  ↓
172	trunk build emits new bundle, new sw.js (different APP_VERSION)
173	  ↓
174	deploy
175	  ↓
176	user reloads (or focuses tab → pwa.js calls registration.update())
177	  ↓
178	browser fetches new sw.js (updateViaCache: "none" guarantees no HTTP cache hit)
179	  ↓
180	new SW installs (precaches new bundle into chinese-chess-pwa-<new-hash>)
181	  ↓
182	new SW state: "installed" + navigator.serviceWorker.controller exists
183	  ↓
184	pwa.js fires CustomEvent("pwa:update-ready")
185	  ↓
186	Leptos PwaUpdateToast shows up
187	  ↓
188	user clicks "Reload"
189	  ↓
190	window.__pwa.applyUpdate() posts {type: "SKIP_WAITING"} to waiting SW
191	  ↓
192	SW calls self.skipWaiting() — becomes the active worker
193	  ↓
194	controllerchange fires on window
195	  ↓
196	pwa.js reloads the page, new bundle takes over.
197	```
198	
199	**Explicit non-features**: we do *not* call `clients.claim()` in
200	the SW, and we do *not* auto-reload. Both would bypass the user's
201	consent and risk reloading the page mid-game.
202	
203	---
204	
205	## Install lifecycle
206	
207	### Chromium (Chrome / Edge / Brave / Samsung Internet / Vivaldi)
208	
209	```
210	user visits site
211	  ↓
212	manifest + SW pass installability checks (HTTPS / localhost,
213	  manifest with name + icons + start_url + display, scope match)
214	  ↓
215	browser fires beforeinstallprompt on window
216	  ↓
217	pwa.js: event.preventDefault() + stash
218	  ↓
219	pwa.js fires CustomEvent("pwa:install-available")
220	  ↓
221	PwaInstallBanner / PwaInstallButton appear
222	  ↓
223	user clicks → window.__pwa.install() → deferredPrompt.prompt()
224	  ↓
225	browser shows native install dialog
226	  ↓
227	user accepts → appinstalled fires → CustomEvent("pwa:installed")
228	  ↓
229	banner / button hide, PwaState.standalone flips true
230	```
231	
232	### iOS Safari (16+)
233	
234	There is **no `beforeinstallprompt`** on iOS. The user has to
235	tap Share → "Add to Home Screen" themselves. We detect iOS via
236	UA + iPadOS-as-Mac-with-touch heuristic and render
237	`PwaInstallBanner` in its `--ios` variant — text + a hint icon
238	rather than a button.
239	
240	### Firefox (desktop)
241	
242	No install prompt at all on desktop Firefox. The banner stays
243	hidden (no `beforeinstallprompt`, not iOS, not standalone).
244	Firefox Android does install via menu → "Install" but doesn't
245	fire `beforeinstallprompt` either; Android Chrome users get the
246	nice flow.
247	
248	### Already installed
249	
250	If `display-mode: standalone` matches at boot, `PwaState.standalone`
251	is `true` and the banner / button stay hidden for the lifetime
252	of that session.
253	
254	---
255	
256	## Local verification recipes
257	
258	### 1. Server mode end-to-end
259	
260	```
261	make build-web
262	cargo run -p chess-net -- --port 7878 \
263	  --static-dir clients/chess-web/dist xiangqi
264	# open http://127.0.0.1:7878/
265	```
266	
267	DevTools checks:
268	
269	- **Application → Manifest**: should be parsed (no errors).
270	  `start_url` = `/`, `scope` = `/`, icons all 200.
271	- **Application → Service Workers**: one registration, source
272	  `http://127.0.0.1:7878/sw.js`, status "activated and is
273	  running". Click "Update on reload" while developing.
274	- **Application → Cache Storage**: one cache named
275	  `chinese-chess-pwa-<12hex>` containing every dist asset.
276	- **Lighthouse PWA audit**: run with "Mobile" emulation. Target
277	  score ≥ 90. Common deductions: missing maskable icon (we
278	  ship one), no offline page (we use the SPA shell fallback,
279	  Lighthouse accepts that).
280	
281	### 2. GitHub Pages mode end-to-end
282	
283	```
284	make serve-web-static WEB_BASE=Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi
285	# open http://localhost:4173/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/
286	```
287	
288	This is the most important verification because the base path
289	is the most error-prone surface. Inspect:
290	
291	```
292	grep start_url clients/chess-web/dist-static/manifest.webmanifest
293	# expect: "start_url": "/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/",
294	
295	grep -o '"/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/[^"]*"' clients/chess-web/dist-static/sw.js | head -5
296	# expect every precache URL prefixed with /Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/
297	```
298	
299	### 3. Offline test
300	
301	```
302	DevTools → Application → Service Workers → check "Offline"
303	Reload the page.
304	Navigate to /local/xiangqi
305	Play 5 moves.
306	```
307	
308	Expected: full functionality. The page loads from cache, the
309	WASM bundle runs, no network requests succeed but the local
310	engine handles everything.
311	
312	If you also wanted online play: DevTools shows the WS connection
313	fails (`/ws/main` cannot reach the server). The lobby page
314	shows the existing "connection failed" UI. Our `OfflineIndicator`
315	in the corner has flipped from green to dim red.
316	
317	### 4. Update test
318	
319	```
320	# Tab open on the previous build.
321	# Edit any file in clients/chess-web/src/
322	make build-web                # or build-web-static
323	# Switch back to the open tab. Reload (Cmd-R).
324	```
325	
326	Expected: **PwaUpdateToast appears**. It does so because:
327	
328	1. The reload triggers `navigator.serviceWorker.register("./sw.js")`
329	   again, which respects `updateViaCache: "none"` and bypasses
330	   HTTP cache to fetch the freshly written `sw.js`.
331	2. The new SW byte-differs from the old (different
332	   `APP_VERSION`), so it installs.
333	3. The new SW enters `waiting` because the old one is still
334	   controlling the page.
335	4. `pwa.js` sees `state === "installed"` plus
336	   `navigator.serviceWorker.controller` non-null and dispatches
337	   `pwa:update-ready`.
338	5. `PwaUpdateToast` subscribed to that event renders.
339	
340	Click the toast. Expected: 1 second later the page reloads
341	running the new bundle.
342	
343	### 5. Mobile install (Android Chrome)
344	
345	```
346	make build-web-static WEB_BASE=Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi
347	make serve-web-static WEB_BASE=Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi
348	```
349	
350	In another terminal find the LAN IP (`ifconfig | grep inet`),
351	then on the phone visit `http://<lan-ip>:4173/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/`.
352	Wait for the picker — `PwaInstallBanner` should appear at the
353	top. Tap "Install"; the OS-level install dialog confirms; the
354	app drawer now has a 帥 icon.
355	
356	> Service workers technically need a secure context, but
357	> `localhost` and (on Android) LAN IPs over plain HTTP are
358	> usually treated as secure for development. For production
359	> you need HTTPS — GitHub Pages provides this automatically.
360	
361	### 6. Mobile install (iOS Safari)
362	
363	Same URL, but the banner shows the iOS variant ("加到主畫面 /
364	Add to Home Screen"). Tap the share icon at the bottom of
365	Safari, scroll to "Add to Home Screen", confirm.
366	
367	---
368	
369	## Replacing the placeholder icons
370	
371	```
372	# Edit clients/chess-web/public/icons/icon.svg          (any-purpose)
373	# Edit clients/chess-web/public/icons/icon-maskable.svg (maskable safe area)
374	clients/chess-web/scripts/regenerate-placeholder-icons.sh
375	# commit the new SVGs + regenerated PNGs
376	```
377	
378	The script uses `rsvg-convert` if available (sharper text), and
379	falls back to ImageMagick. Modern Chrome / Edge / Firefox happily
380	use the SVG entries from the manifest, but iOS Safari only
381	accepts PNG for the apple-touch-icon — that's why the script
382	also rasterizes a 180×180 PNG.
383	
384	**Maskable variant**: Android's adaptive icon system crops up
385	to ~10% off each edge to mask into circles, squircles, etc.
386	Keep all important artwork inside the inner ~80% (a 410×410
387	centred safe area for a 512×512 icon). The committed
388	`icon-maskable.svg` shrinks the disc + glyph relative to the
389	full-bleed background to demonstrate the safe zone.
390	
391	---
392	
393	## How to clear all caches (debugging stale state)
394	
395	DevTools approach:
396	
397	```
398	Application → Storage → "Clear site data" (untick Cookies if
399	you want to keep local prefs).
400	```
401	
402	Manual approach — paste in the console:
403	
404	```js
405	caches.keys().then(keys => Promise.all(keys.map(k => caches.delete(k))));
406	navigator.serviceWorker.getRegistrations()
407	  .then(rs => Promise.all(rs.map(r => r.unregister())));
408	```
409	
410	After running, hard reload (Cmd-Shift-R / Ctrl-Shift-R).
411	
412	---
413	
414	## Future work
415	
416	Tracked in `TODO.md`:
417	
418	- IndexedDB / OPFS persistence for in-progress games (so
419	  closing the tab doesn't lose state).
420	- Web Share API for FEN/PGN of the current position.
421	- Replace placeholder icons with proper artwork.
422	
423	Worth considering if traffic / install count grows:
424	
425	- **Workbox**. We avoided it because hand-written 200-line
426	  `sw.js` is auditable and has zero npm footprint, but Workbox
427	  brings well-tested precaching, navigation routes, expirations,
428	  background sync — worth migrating to if the SW grows past
429	  ~500 lines.
430	- **Background Sync**. Save completed games to a server when
431	  connectivity returns. Requires a server endpoint chess-net
432	  doesn't have today.
433	- **Push Notifications**. Notify when an opponent moves in your
434	  asynchronous correspondence game. Needs Web Push subscription
435	  storage on chess-net. iOS 16.4+ supports this *only* for
436	  installed PWAs.
437	- **Periodic Background Sync**. Useful for prefetching daily
438	  puzzles. Chrome-only, low priority.
439	- **File System Access API**. Save / load PGN files locally
440	  without round-tripping through the server.
441	
442	---
443	
444	## Cross-references
445	
446	- `pitfalls/pwa-base-path-and-stale-cache.md` — the symptoms-
447	  first list of every trap we hit while wiring this up.
448	- `docs/architecture.md` — high-level chess-web architecture;
449	  ADR-0001 explains why presentation lives client-side rather
450	  than in chess-core, which is what makes the SPA installable
451	  in the first place.
452	- `docs/trunk-leptos-wasm.md` — Trunk + Leptos quirks. The
453	  `[[hooks]]` mechanism we use here is documented in the Trunk
454	  manual; see also the existing notes about `data-trunk
455	  rel="copy-file"` not honouring `--public-url` (the post-build
456	  hook side-steps this).
457
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 10:15:25Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Inspect GH Pages workflow

`cat /Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/.github/workflows/pages.yml`

```text
name: pages

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read
  pages: write
  id-token: write

concurrency:
  group: pages
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          targets: wasm32-unknown-unknown
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
      - name: Install Trunk
        run: cargo install trunk --version 0.21.14 --locked
      - name: Build static web
        run: make build-web-static WEB_BASE=/${{ github.event.repository.name }}
      - uses: actions/configure-pages@v6
      - uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v5
        with:
          path: clients/chess-web/dist-static

  deploy:
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment:
      name: github-pages
      url: ${{ steps.deployment.outputs.page_url }}
    steps:
      - id: deployment
        uses: actions/deploy-pages@v5
```
</details></tool-use>

apple-mobile-web-app-title (iOS Safari) in lockstep so the
"Add to Home Screen" label is consistent across platforms.
Adds a "Renaming the app" section to docs/pwa.md so future
renames know to touch every field each platform reads (and
the caveat that already-installed icons keep the old label
until re-install).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 10:21:26Z)**_

全綠 ✅

- `ci` workflow — success (1m27s)
- `pages` workflow — success (1m18s) → GH Pages 應該已經部署完成

接下來實際看到「David's 象棋」的時機:
- **新使用者**:訪問 → 第一次安裝就會看到新名稱。
- **已安裝的舊使用者**:打開 PWA → `PwaUpdateToast` 出現 → 點 Reload 套用新 bundle。**但 home-screen icon 下的字會維持舊的「象棋」**,要移除舊 icon 再重新「加到主畫面」才會變新名稱(iOS / Android 都一樣 — OS 把 home-screen shortcut 的名字當不可變 metadata)。這個 caveat 已經寫進 `docs/pwa.md` 新增的 "Renaming the app" section。
